fault_hypothesis: Line 70 contains a spurious condition that incorrectly removes the last element of `missing` when it equals `replica[-1]`. This is wrong because the element was legitimately added via `extend` (a trailing duplicate in primary that wasn't matched during the merge loop), and should remain in the result.

spec.expr: not (missing__1 and replica and missing__1[-1] == replica[-1])

constraints.intent: The condition that triggers the pop should never be true for valid inputs; when it fires it incorrectly removes a legitimately missing offset.

spec.expr: all(x not in replica for x in missing__1)

constraints.intent: Every element appended to missing during the merge loop must not be present in replica (merge correctness invariant).
After initialization, missing must be empty before the merge loop begins.
The then-branch of the spurious condition should never execute on correct inputs; if it does, a valid missing offset was incorrectly removed.
